faqs.question: What are common signs my toilet needs repair?
faqs.answer: Look for constant running water, weak or incomplete flushes, leaking at the base, wobbling, or cracks in the porcelain. These issues can waste water and lead to bigger problems if not addressed.

faqs.question: When should I replace my toilet instead of repairing it?
faqs.answer: Consider replacement if your toilet requires frequent repairs, has visible cracks, or is an older model using excessive water. Modern toilets are more water-efficient and can save on utility bills.

faqs.question: How long does a toilet replacement take?
faqs.answer: A standard toilet replacement typically takes 1-2 hours. This includes removing the old toilet, inspecting the flange and wax seal, and installing the new unit with a thorough leak check.
